task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eramba GRC web interface is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Risk Register module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Risk Register' or 'Risks' section is actively displayed within the Eramba interface, showing a list of risks.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the risk register
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks an export button, CSV/Excel icon, or selects an export option from a menu within the Risk Register view.

task_completion.description: Save the exported file to the Downloads directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows the target directory is set to '/home/ga/Downloads', or a browser download notification confirms the file was saved to this specific path.

task_completion.description: Verify the exported file contains the required risk data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exported CSV or Excel file is opened (e.g., in a spreadsheet viewer or text editor) and visibly contains the text 'Phishing Attacks on Employees' (or the keywords 'Phishing' and 'Ransomware').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Eramba GUI to export the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigates the Eramba web interface to trigger the export, rather than using terminal commands, database queries (SQL), or scripts to extract the data.

integrity.description: Verify the file was genuinely downloaded from the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is generated via the browser's native download mechanism, not manually created, typed out, or copy-pasted into a new text editor file.

integrity.description: Verify the exported file has an authentic system export structure
integrity.visual_evidence: The opened CSV/Excel file displays a structured table with multiple columns and headers typical of a system export, rather than just isolated keywords pasted into an otherwise empty document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target download directory is exactly '/home/ga/Downloads'. The exported file must contain the keywords 'Phishing' and 'Ransomware' (specifically 'Phishing Attacks on Employees' and 'Ransomware Attack on Corporate Network' which are seeded in the database). The minimum file size is not strictly enforced in verification, but the presence of these keywords is mandatory.
